steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: A linear function $g(x) = mx + b$ where $m$ is the daily weight gain and $b$ is the starting weight. The goat weighs 5 pounds at birth ($x = 0$) and gains $\frac{1}{4}$ pound per day, so $m = \frac{1}{4}$ and $b = 5$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $g(x) = \frac{1}{4}x - 5$: slope is correct but $b = -5$, meaning the goat weighed $-5$ pounds at birth. ✗
  • Choice B — $g(x) = \frac{1}{4}x + 5$: slope $\frac{1}{4}$ matches the daily gain and $b = 5$ matches the birth weight. ✓
  • Choice C — $g(x) = -\frac{1}{4}x - 5$: negative slope means the goat is losing weight every day, and the intercept is negative. Both wrong. ✗
  • Choice D — $g(x) = -\frac{1}{4}x + 5$: intercept is correct but the negative slope means the goat loses $\frac{1}{4}$ pound per day instead of gaining it. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
